title: Dominic Bédard-Lapointe
content:
## Dominic Bédard-Lapointe
People.ContactEmail dbedardlapointe@mccarthy.ca
Known for his strategic insight and rigorous approach, Dominic helps major corporate clients resolve complex tax disputes and achieve favorable outcomes in a rapidly evolving landscape.
Dominic Bédard-Lapointe is a partner in our National Tax Group, based in Montréal. He specializes in tax disputes involving large corporate groups, particularly those resulting from complex reorganizations and the application of the general anti-avoidance rule (GAAR). With deep experience and a nuanced understanding of the tax law, he delivers advice tailored to each clients’ distinctive needs.
Having represented both institutional taxpayers and the Crown, Dominic offers a unique dual perspective that enables him to anticipate challenges and craft efficient, results-oriented litigation strategies. His experience spans precedent-setting cases and significant matters before Canadian courts and tax authorities.
Beyond his practice, Dominic actively contributes to the tax community, teaching in the Master’s in Taxation program at HEC Montréal , presenting at conferences, and publishing extensively. He is also a member of the Montreal chapter of the Canadian Tax Foundation ’s Committee and used to serve as a special advisor to its Board of Governors at the national level.
Dominic was called to the Quebec Bar in 2011 and holds law degrees from HEC Montréal (LL.M., Taxation, 2011) and Université Laval (LL.B., 2008).

#### Representative mandates
- Represented both the Crown and large corporations in dozens of cases concerning tax avoidance, including Husky Energy Inc. v. The King , 2023 TCC 167 (beneficial ownership of shares held by a non-resident), Madison Pacific Properties Inc. v. The King , 2023 TCC 180 (avoidance of acquisition of control), 3295940 Canada Inc. v. The Queen , 2022 TCC 68 (relevance of alternative transactions to establish an abuse of tax laws), Damis Properties Inc. v. The Queen , 2021 TCC 44 (avoidance of subsection 160 in a sale of a corporation), The Gladwin Realty Corporation v. The Queen , 2020 FCA 142; 2019 TCC 62 (optimization of the capital dividend account in a sale of an asset) and Canada v. Alta Energy Luxembourg S.A.R.L. , 2020 FCA 43 (avoidance of Canadian tax on the sale of Canadian resources)
- Represented a petroleum products importer on successfully requesting from the Canadian International Trade Tribunal an extension to file a Notice of Objection, which was required to preserve the company’s capacity to dispute a multimillion dollars excise tax assessment
- Represented Canadian resource companies in making various types of industry specific representations to the Canadian tax authorities as part of the litigation steps that precede the formal court process
- Represented the Crown in various complex cases involving SR&ED tax incentive claimed by Irving Oil (eligibility of SR&ED activities, recognition of relevant costs and tax credits); and
- Represented large corporations in audits and disputes regarding transfer pricing (management fees, guarantees, requests for information) and residence of entities forming part of an international group structure.

#### Publications and conferences
- Bédard-Lapointe, D. and Dolson, M., “GAAR Update: CRA Guidance, Non-Abusive Alternative Transactions and Economic Substance in Practice,” Canadian Tax Foundation Prairie Provinces Tax Conference (Calgary), June 2025
- Bédard-Lapointe, D., Dewey, S. and Dolson, M., “A Practical Discussion on the Mandatory Disclosure Rules,” Tax Executive Institute 57th Annual Canadian Tax Conference (Ottawa), May 2025
- Bédard-Lapointe, D. and Waslowski, J., “Compliance and Planning Certainty: Strategies for Practical Resolution of Unusual Tax Disputes,” 2024 Canadian Tax Foundation 76th Annual Conference (Vancouver), December 2024
- Bédard-Lapointe, D., Verdon, O. and Dompierre M.-F., « Bloc administration fiscale – Panel sur la vérification fiscale et sur les enquêtes criminelles», Annual Conference of the Association de planification fiscale et financière (Gatineau), October 2024
- Bédard-Lapointe, D. and Fitzsimmons, T., “Proposed Increased Canada Revenue Audit Powers,” Tax Executive Institute 76th Anniversary Annual Conference (San Antonio, USA), October 2024

- Goldbach, L., Bédard-Lapointe, D., Meghji, A. and Gheorghiu, L., “The Changing Landscape of Canadian Tax Controversy,” Tax Executive Institute 75th Anniversary Annual Conference (New York, USA), October 2023
- Cloutier, N. and Bédard-Lapointe, D., "Litiges civils à l’encontre de professionnels de la fiscalité : de l’erreur à la faute,", Colloque sur l’administration fiscale of the Association de planification fiscale et financière, March 2023
- Bédard-Lapointe, D., Koshal, A. and Waslowski, J., "How to Document a File - to give yourself protection against litigation," The Canadian Tax Foundation's 74th Annual Tax Conference (Toronto), November 2022
- Dupuis, P. and Bédard-Lapointe, D., "Large Corporations Rules & Subsection 152(9) – Purpose, Safeguards and the Illusion of Unfairness, Tax Disputes in Canada: The Path Forward," Canadian Tax Foundation, December 2022
- Bédard-Lapointe, D., “’Series of Transactions’: Has its Expansion Halted?” Canadian Tax Focus, Canadian Tax Foundation, Volume 11, Number 2, 2021
- Bédard-Lapointe, D. and Cardinal, M., Acquisition de contrôle : impacts sur les attributs fiscaux et évitement des restrictions à leur utilisation, Canadian Tax Foundation luncheon seminar, January 2020
- Bédard-Lapointe, D. and Lafrance, J., Jurisprudence récente en matière de planification, de réorganisation, de fiscalité internationale et d’évitement d’impôt, Canadian Tax Foundation luncheon seminar, December 2018
- Bédard-Lapointe, D., Considérations pratiques relatives à l’application de la règle générale anti-évitement, annual conference of the Association de planification fiscale et financière, October 2017
- Bédard-Lapointe, D., Capital versé : Aspect légal et fiscal, continuing education program of the Association de planification fiscale et financière, 2016-2021
- Lévesque, É. and Bédard-Lapointe, D., Anatomie d’un contrat d’achat d’actifs – Les principales dispositions d’intérêt pour le fiscaliste, annual conference of the Association de planification fiscale et financière, October 2015
- Lévesque, É. and Bédard-Lapointe, D., Anatomie d’un contrat d’achat d’actions – Les principales dispositions d’intérêt pour le fiscaliste, annual conference of the Association de planification fiscale et financière, October 2014.
